t_ch: 你还不知道吧，伙计？我和我的团队已经为匹诺康尼这个城建项目工作很久了。
t_en: You know what? My team and I have been working on this development project in Penacony for quite some time.

t_ch: 不瞒你说，奇迹本杰明过去一段时间的口碑稍微有些…动荡。
t_en: Frankly speaking, Miracle Benjamin Studio has got a... bad rap recently...

t_ch: 不只是在朋克洛德内部，其他星系的很多老粉丝都在批评我们不思进取，只会抱着几个老IP不放，天天吃老本。
t_en: Our long-time fans from both Punklorde and other star systems have been criticizing us for resting on our laurels and not having the drive to innovate something new.

t_ch: 而且，呃…不知道你听说过没有，我们的上一个大项目——《末世班尼：烈火英雄》——发售的时候出了一点小小的，呃…意外。
t_en: Also, um... I'm not sure whether you've heard about the... um... little mishap that took place at the launch of our last major project, Apocalyptic Benny: Hero Baptised In Fire.

t_ch: 我就直说了吧，之所以接下匹诺康尼这个活，就是因为奇迹本杰明需要打一场漂亮的翻身仗，而且必须是一场大胜仗才行！
t_en: Let me be straight with you — I accepted this job in Penacony because our studio needed a successful project to turn this all around!

t_ch: 借助匹诺康尼这个平台，我们一定可以大展拳脚…这座梦中城市将会是奇迹本杰明迄今为止最伟大的作品，毫不夸张。所有的新老粉丝都会迷上它的，我保证。
t_en: We believe we can unleash our full potential in Penacony... This city of dreams is bound to be the greatest work for Miracle Benjamin Studio. I'm not exaggerating that. I'm sure our fans, both new and old, will be mesmerized by it.

t_ch: 不过，呃…为了确保我们的工作成果达到了每个人的预期，我还需要…呃…做一些调研工作。
t_en: Still, um... to make sure our work meets everyone's expectations, I need to... um... do some research.

t_ch: 调研？
t_en: Research?

t_ch: 听上去轮到我干活了。
t_en: Sounds like my job.

t_ch: 你，伙计！你正好是我需要的新面孔！
t_en: You! You happen to be the fresh face that I need!

t_ch: 我需要一个没那么脸熟的人帮我收集信息。我得知道在那些个家族成员眼里，我们干的这些活到底有多少价值。
t_en: I need a fresh face to gather information for me, as I want to find out how The Family values our work.

t_ch: 如果可能的话，最好让他们打个具体的分数…至于具体该找谁打听，你自己定吧。放心，凡是<color=#dbc291ff>家族里稍微有点地位的人</color>应该都已经来过这里、体验过一遍了。
t_en: If possible, I'd like them to rate our work... It's up to you who you want to approach. Don't worry, as <color=#dbc291ff>anyone who holds a position in The Family</color> should have already been here and tried what we have to offer.

t_ch: 全靠你了，伙计！
t_en: I'll count on you then, buddy!

t_ch: 好伙计，这个任务就交给你了！我需要一个新面孔帮我收集信息，看看我们搞的这个项目在他们看来有多少价值。
t_en: The mission is yours, buddy! I need a fresh face to gather information for me, as I want to find out how The Family values our work.

t_ch: 如果可能的话，最好让他们打个具体的分数…至于具体该找谁打听，你自己定吧。放心，凡是<color=#dbc291ff>家族里稍微有点地位的人</color>应该都已经来过这里、体验过一遍了。
t_en: If possible, I'd like them to rate our work... It's up to you who you want to approach. Don't worry, as <color=#dbc291ff>anyone who holds a position in The Family</color> should have already been here and tried what we have to offer.

t_ch: 你对筑梦边境项目怎么看？
t_en: What are your thoughts on the Dream's Edge project?

t_ch: 「筑梦边境」？哦…我想起来了，就是那个被内部吹上天的城区拓建计划，对吧？
t_en: The Dream's Edge? Oh... I remember it. It's the city expansion project that has been lauded to the skies by The Family, right?

t_ch: 那些整天吹捧的家伙，我真怀疑他们到底有没有亲自去那地方转过一圈。反正我去过了，感觉一般。
t_en: For those who are full of praise, I doubt whether they have really been there. I visited the place, and I must say I'm not impressed.

t_ch: 怎么个「一般」法？
t_en: How so?

t_ch: 非要说的话，就是「平庸」吧？转了一大圈，没发现什么亮点。
t_en: To be more precise, the place is just mediocre. I didn't see anything special when I was there.

t_ch: 我也觉得一般。
t_en: I agree.

t_ch: 嗯？你又不是家族的人，是怎么……
t_en: Eh? You aren't part of The Family. How did you...

t_ch: …咳，算了，反正也不关我事。总而言之，那座新城给我的感觉就是「平庸」。
t_en: ...Ahem, forget it. It's none of my business. Anyway, that new city is nothing but mediocre, if you ask me.

t_ch: 现在来到匹诺康尼的游客，口味已经被养得很刁了。睁大眼睛看看吧，这个奢侈至极的「黄金的时刻」——就这还有人嫌玩不够呢。
t_en: Visitors who come to Penacony nowadays are very fussy. Just take a look at Golden Hour. I can't believe there are people who are sick of this extravagant place.

t_ch: 我听说那个叫班尼的负责人夸下海口，说是要打造一个闻所未闻的梦境都市。但是在我看来，他和他的团队鼓捣的还是老的那套东西，换汤不换药罢了。
t_en: I heard that Benny guy, the head of the project, boasted about creating a dreamscape metropolis that had never been seen before. But what he and his team are churning out seems nothing new to me. It's just old wine in a new bottle.

t_ch: 现在的逐梦客，什么场面没见过？只靠生硬堆料吸引眼球的时代已经过去了…奇迹本杰明的那套做法过时了。
t_en: Dreamchasers nowadays have seen it all. Gone are the days when you can easily grab attention by piling different ideas together stiffly... Miracle Benjamin's style is just outdated.

t_ch: 给它打个分吧。
t_en: Please rate his work.

t_ch: 啊？怎么还有打分环节？
t_en: Huh? I didn't know I had to do that.

t_ch: 一定要说的话…我给7分吧。如果是已经习惯了班尼那套虚拟现实设计思路的游客，说不定会挺乐在其中的？
t_en: If I must give his work a score... it's probably a seven-out-of-ten. Visitors who are used to Benny's usual virtual reality design might lap it up, right?

t_ch: 毕竟有黄金的时刻铺下的底子，在这个基础上换个皮也不至于有多差。
t_en: After all, it's built with Golden Hour as its foundation. The result shouldn't be too bad.

t_ch: 真让人感慨啊——我还记得好多年前，《班尼古堡》横空出世的时候，我当时还采访过奇迹本杰明工作室。那个时候他们可真是行业里的翘楚。
t_en: Years ago when Benny's Castle was released, I even did an interview with Miracle Benjamin Studio. At that time, they were indeed a leader in the industry.

t_ch: 现在…唉，可能人的理想和才气是真的会被燃尽的吧？
t_en: Now... Alas, it might be true that one's ambition and talent will one day be exhausted.

t_ch: 您对筑梦边境项目怎么看？
t_en: What do you think of the Dream's Edge project?

t_ch: 啊，有趣。看来有些人很想知道家族对他们工作成果的看法…但又碍于面子不想亲自询问。人之常情。
t_en: Ah, how amusing. It seems like some people are keen to know how The Family views their work but have too much pride to ask themselves. This is human nature.

t_ch: 奇迹本杰明——这个团队在银河间久负盛名，我听说过他们以往的作品和成就。
t_en: Miracle Benjamin is a team that has been well-known for quite some time in the cosmos. I've heard of their past work and accomplishments.

t_ch: 过去半个纪，奇迹本杰明证明了自己有能力打造令人印象深刻的虚拟现实世界。家族确信这样一个团队足以胜任匹诺康尼新城区筑梦师。
t_en: Over the past half Amber Era, Miracle Benjamin has proven itself to be capable of creating memorable virtual reality worlds. The Family believes that a team like that has what it takes to be the Dreamweavers of Penacony's new city.

t_ch: 但我并不抱有太过高远的期望。家族选中了这个团队来进行新城区建设，更多是看中了他们身上的…「噱头」。
t_en: However, my hopes aren't high. The Family's decision to hire this team is more of a gimmick.

t_ch: 时代已经变了。「噱头」在某种程度上已经比「实质」更加重要。而班尼——
t_en: Times have changed. To some extent, a good gimmick is all that matters, but not the substance. As for Benny...

t_ch: 话说到一半，惠特克爵士冷笑了一声。
t_en: Sir Whittaker sneers.

t_ch: ——他是个擅长制造「噱头」的人。
t_en: ...He's a master at creating gimmicks.

t_ch: 展开说说？
t_en: Care to elaborate?

t_ch: 他平时好像确实很亢奋…
t_en: He seems excited all the time...

t_ch: 我不想过度深入细节，只不过……
t_en: I do not wish to go into the details, however...

t_ch: 上次他在公开发布会上做出承诺的时候，结果可不怎么好看。
t_en: It didn't end up well when he made that promise at the public press conference.

t_ch: 你还没说对这项工程的看法。
t_en: You haven't shared your views about the project.

t_ch: 我的评价？符合预期。
t_en: My views? Well, it meets my expectations.

t_ch: 等到这座城区结束施工，真正向逐梦客开放的那天，围绕它的评价将是这般——
t_en: By the time the city is built and ready to welcome Dreamchasers, this is what I think will happen:

t_ch: ——小部分盲目吹捧的拥趸将会被舆论攻击；小部分等待口碑反噬的看客会开始狂欢。而对于其他人——也是占到百分之九十的大多数而言——他们不会在乎。
t_en: Some will come under fire for blindly supporting the project, and some will be sitting on the edge of their seats, waiting to see the studio's reputation take a nosedive. As for the rest, which makes up to ninety percent of the public, they simply won't care.

t_ch: 他们会来到这里，继续消费、享乐。他们从来不在乎所谓的精益求精，他们需要的只是某种程度上的新鲜感。
t_en: They will come here to splurge and have a good time as usual. They never care about improvement. Anything new will be enough to meet their needs.

t_ch: 7分是个合理的评价，符合家族的需求，也符合奇迹本杰明当下的水准。班尼或许会极力否认…但这就是无可辩驳的事实。
t_en: Seven would be a reasonable score. The project fulfills the needs of The Family and reflects the current standard of Miracle Benjamin. Benny might argue to the contrary, but it's an irrefutable fact.

t_ch: 你怎么评价筑梦边境项目？
t_en: What do you think about the Dream's Edge project?
Hmm...

t_ch: 没有对任何人不敬的意思，但这个工程整个就…缺乏想象力。
t_en: With all due respect, that entire project... lacks imagination.

t_ch: 依我之见，那个团队的筑梦师，工作的过程就像是在套公式。他们不是在搞创作，更像是在解几何题。
t_en: From the way I see it, their Dreamweavers seem to follow a fixed formula when they work. When it's supposed to be a creative process, they may look more like they are solving a geometric problem.

t_ch: 这可能算不上什么大问题。但最让我受不了的就是…就是……
t_en: That's not the deal-breaker. What truly makes me sick is...

t_ch: 是什么？
t_en: What is it?

t_ch: …就是他们对「门」的执着。
t_en: ...Their obsession with doors.

t_ch: 你只要去过一次就明白了——那城区里哪哪儿都是门！进个大楼要过门，上个天桥要过门，就连顶楼的空中花园都要推门才能进去。
t_en: You'll get what I mean when you visit the place. Doors are everywhere in the city! You have to pass through a door to enter a building, cross a pedestrian bridge, and access the rooftop garden.

t_ch: 他们为什么对门这么执着？我反正理解不了。如果没人出来解释，我就只能理解为技术有限，能力不足了。
t_en: What's the reason behind the obsession? I'm puzzled. If no one cares to explain, I'll take it as a sign of incompetence.

t_ch: 满分十分，给个分数吧。
t_en: Please give it a score out of ten.
Hmm...

t_ch: 那我就给个…七分吧。不得罪人，也算得上客观。
t_en: I'll give it a... seven. It isn't too harsh and is fair.

t_ch: 我听说那个筑梦师团队以前整出过不少风评不错的虚拟现实项目。谁知道呢？指不定有些人就吃这套。
t_en: I've been told that the team used to produce many critically acclaimed virtual reality projects back then. Who knows? Some people might be drawn to it.

t_ch: 好伙计，这个任务就交给你了！我需要一个新面孔帮我收集信息，看看我们搞的这个项目在他们看来有多少价值。
t_en: The mission is yours, buddy! I need a fresh face to gather information for me, as I want to find out how The Family values our work.

t_ch: 如果可能的话，最好让他们打个具体的分数…至于具体该找谁打听，你自己定吧。放心，凡是家族里稍微有点地位的人应该都已经来过这里、体验过一遍了。
t_en: If possible, I'd like them to rate our work... It's up to you who you want to approach. Don't worry, as anyone who holds a position in The Family should have already been here and tried what we have to offer.

t_ch: 我收集到家族的评价了。
t_en: I've collected the ratings from some Family members.

t_ch: 靠谱，伙计，真不枉费我对你的信任！
t_en: You're so reliable, buddy! I knew I could trust you!

t_ch: 来，说给我听听吧，让我看看家族的人到底是怎么想的。
t_en: Now, tell me everything. Let me see what they think of my project.
Seven... Seven!?

t_ch: …你确定这是他们真实的想法吗，伙计？你没给他们灌输什么主观意见吧？
t_en: ...Are you sure the scores come from the bottom of their hearts? You didn't say anything that swayed their opinions, did you?

t_ch: 这么干对我有什么好处？
t_en: Why would I want to do that?

t_ch: 这锅可不能扣我头上啊…
t_en: Don't pin this on me...

t_ch: 无法接受…我真的不能接受！
t_en: This is outrageous... I can't accept it!

t_ch: 这…你看看他们这一个个的，给的算是什么理由？我的团队付出了这么多时间和心血，他们就不能表达出一点儿感激之心吗？
t_en: Look at the ridiculous things they said about my project! My team and I are so committed to this project. Why can't they just be grateful for what we've done?

t_ch: 这是谁说的，「平庸」？说这话的人真的懂行吗…我们要打造的可是领先于时代的东西，我看她还是先去提升下自己的审美再说吧！
t_en: Who made this comment? Mediocre!? Does she even know anything about the industry? We're making something ahead of this era here. She should improve her tastes before making any comments!

t_ch: 这人是什么意思…奇迹本杰明带来的更多是「噱头」，而非「实质」？
t_en: What does this guy mean about Miracle Benjamin being more of a gimmick than substance?

t_ch: 简直离谱！他这是在指控我们虚假宣传吗？我们的宣传物料里说的可是句句属实。不信你让他来数，这里到底是不是有上千座高楼！
t_en: It's downright nonsense! Is he accusing us of false advertising? We mean everything we include in the advertisement. He's welcome to come here and count whether there are indeed a thousand buildings here!

t_ch: 对「门」过于执着…？这算什么评价，在逗我吗？要不让说这话的人把他家里的门都拆了如何，看看他的小日子还能过吗？
t_en: We're obsessed with doors? What kind of a comment is that? Is it a joke? How about he remove all the doors in his house and see whether it makes his life better?

t_ch: 家族这群两幅面孔的货，明面上笑脸相迎，背后居然在狠狠地嚼我们的舌根……
t_en: These two-faced scumbags from The Family! They greet us with smiling faces but criticize us behind our backs...

t_ch: 不尊重我可以，但要是不尊重我的团队、还有我们辛苦产出的作品，那就越界了！
t_en: They can humiliate me, but they mustn't disrespect my team and our hard work! That would be crossing the line!

t_ch: 这样下去绝对不行，我得去找家族的人评评理。有什么不满意的地方就不能直说吗？暗搓搓地在背后等着看你的笑话，这叫什么事？这可是他们自己的项目！
t_en: No way. I need to talk to them and sort things out. Why can't they speak up forthrightly if there's something they aren't happy with? Are they secretly waiting to see me making a fool out of myself? Have they forgotten this is also their project?

t_ch: 多谢你，伙计。要是没有你帮上这一手，我怕是等到城区交付的那天都要被蒙在鼓里。这些代币你拿去吧，不用客气！
t_en: Thank you so much, buddy. If it weren't for your help, I might have been kept in the dark until the day I delivered the project. Please keep these tokens of my appreciation.

t_ch: 家族的人请我们来建设城市，然后又要在背后对我们的工作评头论足……
t_en: The Family members hired us to build the city, but now they're nitpicking our work behind our back...

t_ch: 这样的合作方式绝对行不通。我要去找家族管事的聊聊，看看他们究竟有什么高见！
t_en: This kind of collaboration isn't healthy. I need to talk to their supervisor and see what he has to say!

t_ch: …是谁？
t_en: ...You know us?

t_ch: 哦…是你啊，伙计。
t_en: Oh, it's you, buddy.

t_ch: 看着兴致不高啊。
t_en: You seem to be in low spirits.

t_ch: …有那么明显吗？一下就被你看出来了。
t_en: ...Is it that obvious? You see right through me at one glance.

t_ch: 你和家族聊过了？
t_en: Have you talked to The Family?

t_ch: 嗯…聊过了。
t_en: Yes...

t_ch: 这事确实是我冲动了——不管怎么说，人家才是金主。脑子里怎么想的不重要，还得是合同上的白纸黑字才有效力。
t_en: Well, I acted a little rashly... After all, they're the boss. What they think doesn't matter, as long as they fulfill their obligations on the contract.

t_ch: 何况宣发费用都是人家掏的腰包，他们也确实给我们争取到了延期建设的时间……
t_en: Moreover, they did spring for the promotional expenses and gave us more time to complete the project...

t_ch: …别用那种眼神看我！我们…奇迹本杰明团队，会完成应尽的职责，建设好这座城市。
t_en: ...Don't give me that look! We, the Miracle Benjamin team, will build a fine city, as per our duties.

t_ch: 就算不被外界看好，我们也必须相信自己。没错，我们正在做的事情是历史性的，开创性的……
t_en: Even though no one thinks highly of us, we must believe in ourselves. What we're doing is unprecedented and is going to make history...

t_ch: 骗谁也别骗了自己。
t_en: You can lie to whomever you want, but not yourself.

t_ch: …你是懂聊天的，伙计。
t_en: ...You're quite a conversationalist, buddy.

t_ch: 好好干，加油。
t_en: Bring your A-game.

t_ch: 对…是得好好干才行。说了等于没说，伙计。
t_en: Right... I must do my best, but that's not helping, buddy.

t_ch: 不可理喻…太可恶了！多少个日夜的辛勤劳作，居然被家族那群人两三句话就都给毁了！
t_en: This is outlandish... How infuriating! They negate all our hard work with their off-the-cuff remarks!

t_ch: 放到以前，《班尼古堡》刚上市的时候，谁敢质疑我们的能力？唉…想当年，光靠每年推出一个新版本都足够维持团队生计了……
t_en: Back in the days when Benny's Castle was just released, who would have doubted our abilities? At that time, we had enough to sustain the team by just releasing a new game version every year...

t_ch: …让我自己待会儿吧，伙计。我得静下来好好构想一下未来的方向……
t_en: ...Please leave me be for a while, buddy. I need some time to ponder about our future...

t_ch: 让我一个人待会儿，伙计…我现在没心情开玩笑了，真的。
t_en: Please let me be with myself for a while, buddy. I'm not in the mood to joke around.

t_ch: 你知道要在每个宣传片里都装出自信满满的样子有多难吗？没经历过的人是不会懂的……
t_en: Do you know how hard it is to pretend to be confident in every promotional video? Those who haven't tried won't understand it...

t_ch: 班尼的眼神黯淡了下来，举手投足间的自信气质刹那间消失得无影无踪。
t_en: Benny's eyes have lost their usual glint, and he no longer exudes his typical air of confidence.

t_ch: 看来我们的时代真的已经过去了，伙计……
t_en: Looks like our time has truly come to an end, buddy...

t_ch: 我也不知道到底哪儿出问题了。团队还是那个团队，我也还是那个我…这个市场真的已经把我们抛弃了吗？
t_en: I have no idea what went wrong. Everything is the same, be it the team or me... Have we really been abandoned by the market?

t_ch: 我不知道，伙计…我真的想不太通。我们已经没退路了，下一个项目肯定会决定奇迹本杰明的生死……
t_en: I'm at my wit's end, buddy... There's no turning back for us now. The next project will decide Miracle Benjamin's fate...

t_ch: 我又仔细想了一想…7分好像也不是那么难接受的评价。
t_en: Actually, seven isn't that bad after I've given it some thought.

t_ch: 随便让外面的人说去吧…我相信我的团队。我们只管稳步把项目做完就行，别的什么都别想。
t_en: The outsiders can say whatever they want... I have faith in my team. We should focus on completing the project without letting other things distract us.

t_ch: 班尼陷入了一阵思考，他的沉稳冷静令你觉得有些不适应。
t_en: Benny is absorbed in his thoughts. You aren't used to his composure and calmness.

t_ch: 我又仔细想了一想…7分好像也不是那么难接受的评价。
t_en: Actually, seven isn't that bad after I've given it some thought.

t_ch: 奇迹本杰明名声在外，哪怕评价褒贬不一，也还是会有大批逐梦客被我们的招牌吸引来的。
t_en: Miracle Benjamin is already a household name. Even if there are mixed reviews about our work, I believe a lot of Dreamchasers will still be drawn here because of our reputation.

t_ch: 我还记得，《末世班尼：烈火英雄》——我们的上一款虚拟现实模拟器——刚上线的时候简直就是灾难。那回我们都挺过来了，还有什么可怕的？
t_en: The launch of our last virtual reality simulator, Apocalyptic Benny: Hero Baptised In Fire, was a disaster in the beginning, but we managed to pull through. What's there to be afraid of this time?

t_ch: 随便让外面的人说去吧…我相信我的团队。我们只管稳步把项目做完就行，别的什么都别想。
t_en: The outsiders can say whatever they want... I have faith in my team. We should focus on completing the project without letting other things distract us.

t_ch: 往好处想，伙计，这个项目能帮我们累积足够的开发资金，让我们去做真正的大事。
t_en: Look on the bright side, buddy. We'll be able to earn enough capital through this project to fund what we really want to do.

t_ch: 奇迹本杰明一定会逆转风评！我们要把所有资源都投入到我们的下一个项目——《班尼古堡：海底新生》。看扁我们的人，都给我等着吧！
t_en: We'll turn the table eventually! We're going to channel all our resources into our next project: Benny's Castle: Seabed Rebirth and prove those who look down on us wrong!

t_ch: 班尼两眼放光，神态让你想起自己和他初识时的样子。
t_en: Benny's eyes are gleaming. His demeanor reminds you of how he looked when you first met him.

t_ch: 今天是个好日子，伙计！把那些不愉快的事统统忘掉吧——头脑转起来！手臂动起来！
t_en: Today is a good day, buddy! Let's put all the bad things behind us! Move those gears in your head! Get those arms moving!

t_ch: 我们可是奇迹本杰明的筑梦师，匹诺康尼的建设者。我们在打造一项前人闻所未闻的事业！
t_en: We're the Dreamweavers from Miracle Benjamin. We're going to make history in Penacony!

t_ch: 他们不懂我们的工作有多么伟大…那是他们的问题。就像那些抱怨我们的虚拟现实产品不够「真实」的家伙一样——伙计，或许你们该考虑升级一下自己的设备了。
t_en: If they can't see the greatness of our work, it's their problem, just like those guys who complain that our virtual reality products aren't "real" enough. It's probably time they upgrade their parts.

t_ch: 多往好处想，这个项目能帮我们累积足够的开发资金，支持我们未来去做真正的大事。
t_en: Let's look on the bright side. We'll be able to earn enough capital through this project to fund what we really want to do.

t_ch: 奇迹本杰明一定会逆转风评！我们要把所有资源都投入到我们的下一个项目——《班尼古堡：海底新生》。看扁我们的人，都给我等着吧！
t_en: We'll turn the table eventually! We're going to channel all our resources into our next project: Benny's Castle: Seabed Rebirth and prove those who look down on us wrong!

t_ch: 我奋斗了大几十年，把奇迹本杰明从一间小工作室带到了前人想都不敢想的高度……
t_en: With decades of hard work, I've transformed Miracle Benjamin from a small studio to a level that my predecessors could never have imagined...

t_ch: …外人谁都别想对我指手画脚！别想！
t_en: ...No one is in the position to speak ill of my accomplishments! Nobody!

t_ch: 班尼的嘴角开始不自然地抽搐。看得出来，他平日里大概是个随和的人，不常发火。
t_en: The corners of Benny's mouth start twitching. You can tell he's an easygoing guy who rarely loses his cool.

t_ch: 大概一百多年以前吧，我还在上学的时候，一群小屁孩聚在一块聊过未来的梦想——
t_en: About a century ago when I was still a student, my friends and I gathered and talked about our dreams.

t_ch: ——其他孩子说的都是运动员、政客、商业大亨什么的，只有我说：「我要当虚拟现实设计师，打造所有人都能沉浸其中的完美体验！」
t_en: While most of them aspired to be athletes, politicians, or businessmen, I was the only one who wanted to be a virtual reality designer who could create a perfect and immersive world for everyone.

t_ch: 当时在场的人都在笑。他们觉得我是个呆子，只配缩在小黑屋里下我的星际战棋——现在再看看，功成名就的是谁？笑到最后的是谁？
t_en: Everyone laughed at me, thinking I was a dork who only spent my time playing galactic warchess at home —but see who's the successful one now? Who has the last laugh?

t_ch: 我奋斗了大几十年，把奇迹本杰明从一间小工作室带到了前人想都不敢想的高度……
t_en: With decades of hard work, I've transformed Miracle Benjamin from a small studio to a level that my predecessors could never have imagined...

t_ch: …外人谁都别想对我指手画脚！别想！
t_en: ...No one is in the position to speak ill of my accomplishments! Nobody!

t_ch: 「梦想之城」？呵…那怎么就没人关心关心我的梦想呢……
t_en: The City of Dreams? Hmph... How about my dream?

t_ch: …嗯？哎呀，您好…这么巧遇见，不如来一瓶？
t_en: ...Eh? What a coincidence to run into you here. Shall we have a drink?

t_ch: 您瞧，这可是——可贩售的美、美梦，液态的谐乐颂，哈哈…哈哈哈……
t_en: Look, th—this is... the d—dream we can buy... The liquid form of Odes of Harmony, haha... hahaha...

t_ch: 汽水也能喝醉？
t_en: How did he get drunk from soda?

t_ch: 这…有什么稀奇的，我们可是、可是活在梦里，哈哈……
t_en: What's... special about that? D—Don't forget we're... living in a dream, haha...

t_ch: 您看这梦…它这——么大！这——么金碧辉煌！
t_en: Look at this dream... It's SOOOO big! It's SOOOO grand!

t_ch: 这么…大…怎么、怎么就容不下我那点小愿望呢……
t_en: Since it's so... big... H—How come my small wish can't fit in it...

t_ch: 小孩子才喝汽水……
t_en: Soda is for kids...

t_ch: 嗯嗯…您说的对，小孩子…哈哈，要真是小孩子就好了……
t_en: Hmm... You're right. Kids... haha. If only I were a kid...

t_ch: 小孩子的梦里都是欢声笑语，哪像我…不像我……
t_en: Kids only have happy dreams, unlike... unlike me...

t_ch: 快，给我来一瓶。
t_en: Come on. Get me a bottle.

t_ch: 哈哈，看您就是个懂的…所谓醉生梦死……
t_en: Haha, I can tell you're my kind of guy... Life is all about...

t_ch: 不就是醉的人生、梦的人死吗…哈哈哈！
t_en: Having a good drink and failed dreams... Hahaha!

t_ch: 相信我，您保准会爱上这玩意！
t_en: Trust me. You'll be hooked on this drink!

t_ch: 这苏、苏乐达啊…一口开怀、三口忘忧、十口入喉不知愁…哈哈…只要您喝得够多——
t_en: This is S—SoulGlad... One sip will cheer you up, three will make you forget about troubles, and ten will send you on cloud nine... Haha, as long as you down enough of it...

t_ch: 什么「被上司欺压的愤怒」啊、什么被「同事排挤的愁苦」啊…就连「即将到手的绩效被人横刀夺走」这种事，都会融化在糖浆和气泡里啦！
t_en: Your rage of being bullied by your boss, your bitterness of being ostracized by your co-workers, or even your indignation of getting your sales stolen by others will dissolve in the syrup and bubbles!

t_ch: 要是您…嘿嘿…要是您不嫌弃我这个醉鬼…就陪我聊几句吧？就几句，求您啦！
t_en: If you... hee-hee... don't find my tipsy state off-putting, you're welcome... to chat with me. Just for a while, please!

t_ch: 我、我请…嗝…请您喝苏乐达…管够！
t_en: I, I will... *burp*... buy you some SoulGlad... You can have as much as you want!

t_ch: 你想聊什么？
t_en: What do you want to talk about?

t_ch: 您、您真的愿意陪我聊天吗？您…您可真是个好人！不过……
t_en: A—Are you really willing to chat with me? Y—You must have a heart of gold! Still...

t_ch: 我啊，一直以来，要听的太多，能讲的太少。
t_en: I've been listening to others so much... that I have little... to talk about myself.

t_ch: 以至于现在突然有人愿意陪我说说话，我反而不知该讲什么好了。实在是抱歉……
t_en: Now that there's finally someone willing to listen to me, I have no idea what to say. Very sorry about that...

t_ch: 你是谁？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 哈——这可真是个好问题！有人叫我「空想家」，有人叫我「麻烦精」、还有人叫我「坏事的」…您觉得哪个比较好听？
t_en: Aha, that's a great question! Some call me delusional, some call me a troublemaker, and some call me a loose cannon... Which nickname do you think is the best?

t_ch: 你觉得我觉得呢？
t_en: What do you think?

t_ch: 啊…哦！抱、抱歉…我不是…我没想要阴阳怪气您…只不过每次他们这么问我，都是想要挖苦我……
t_en: Ah... Oh! I—I'm sorry... I didn't... It wasn't a sarcastic comment... It's just that every time they ask me questions like that, it's to make fun of me...

t_ch: 总、总之…我叫波奇，是星际和平公司的员工，隶属于市场开拓部。
t_en: A—Anyway... My name is Bocchi, and I work in the Marketing Development Department of the IPC.

t_ch: 虽然还只是个刚步入职场的新人，但我的梦想是成为像奥斯瓦尔多主管那样的成功人士！
t_en: Although I'm just a rookie now, I aspire to become as successful as Oswaldo, head of my department, one day!

t_ch: 即便现在遇到了些小…挫折…好吧，这个挫折对我来说或许并没有那么小……
t_en: Even though I'm facing a minor... setback... Fine, it isn't exactly minor for me...

t_ch: 你这么喜欢苏乐达？
t_en: Are you a fan of SoulGlad?

t_ch: …是啊，谁能拒绝气泡丰盈的美味糖浆呢。不过嘛，苏乐达真正吸引我的……
t_en: ...For sure I am. Who could resist this bubbly, delicious syrup? Actually, there's something else about SoulGlad that truly appeals to me...

t_ch: 您听过苏萨先生吗？就是那个苏乐达的创始人。
t_en: Have you heard of Mr. Sousa, the inventor of SoulGlad?

t_ch: 那是谁？
t_en: Who's that?

t_ch: 苏萨先生他…是位厨艺高超的大善人，在匹诺康尼还是颗监狱星的时候，为了救赎犯人只身来到这里。
t_en: Mr. Sousa was a philanthropist and also an amazing cook. At the time when Penacony was still a prison planet, Mr. Sousa came here by himself to help the prisoners.

t_ch: 为了改善那些可怜人的生活，他用狱中简陋的食材与传说中的「梦乐草」，调配出苏乐达这种甜美绝伦的饮品。
t_en: To improve the life of the miserable bunch, he used the humble ingredients found in the prison and the legendary Dreamleaves to produce a sugary and yummy concoction — SoulGlad.

t_ch: 有点印象。
t_en: The name sounds familiar.

t_ch: 果然您也听说过他，我相信这位大善人的美名迟早会响彻寰宇！
t_en: I knew you would have heard of him! Sooner or later, he'll be famous across the entire universe!

t_ch: 虽说苏萨先生因触动了…呃，某些坏家伙的利益而遭到迫害，就连梦乐草都因此惨遭灭绝，但无论是苏乐达，还是他的理念、他的传说——这份传承始终都未曾断绝！
t_en: Mr. Sousa was later persecuted for, um, undermining the interests of some bad guys, and even the Dreamleaves went extinct. But SoulGlad and his story have passed down from generation to generation ever since.

t_ch: 「希望某日宇宙中的所有人都能坐在一张桌子上，快乐地共饮苏乐达。」
t_en: "I hope that one day, everyone in the universe can enjoy SoulGlad at the same table."

t_ch: 多美的梦啊！希望有朝一日，我的梦想也能像这位伟人的梦想一样，被人们所铭记！
t_en: What a beautiful dream! I wish my dream could be remembered by everyone just like Mr. Sousa's!

t_ch: 不打扰你了。
t_en: I won't bother you.

t_ch: 不不不，一点都不打扰！我还巴不得多和您聊几句呢！
t_en: No, you aren't bothering me at all! I couldn't be happier to carry on this conversation!

t_ch: 好久都没和人聊得这么开心了，唉，要是早点认识您该多好……
t_en: It's been a long time since I had such an enjoyable chat. Alas, if only I met you earlier...

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 您要是…要是什么时候得了空，能不能再来这边转转？我、我请您喝苏乐达，管够！
t_en: If you... you're free next time, could you drop by again? I—I'll get you some SoulGlad, as much as you can drink!

t_ch: 一醉解千愁，无梦便无忧…嘿，我果然该去当个诗人……
t_en: I drink away my sorrow at night, so that I will have no dreams and sleep tight... I really should become a poet...

t_ch: 啊！痛、好痛…是…喝得太猛了吗…脑袋…脑袋好像要炸开了……
t_en: Ouch! It... It hurts... Is it because I've drunk too much... My head... My head is splitting...

t_ch: …咦…奇怪…真奇怪，怎么突然…啊——原来是这样！
t_en: ...Eh... This... This is weird. That happened all of a sudden... Oh... I see!

t_ch: 你还好吗？
t_en: Are you okay?

t_ch: 我？我很好，简直不能再好了——我终于明白了，哈、哈哈！
t_en: Me? I'm good. Never better! I've finally got it! Hahaha!

t_ch: 您知道吗，我曾几乎就要踏上成功之路…可那条路并非坦途，总有些卑鄙的垄断者、贪婪的投机者守在路口，死死叼住嘴里的肥肉，生怕别人分去一星油渣……
t_en: You know what? I was once on the verge of success... Yet, it wasn't without challenges. There were always some despicable monopolists and greedy speculators lurking at the crossroads, tightly holding onto the juicy bits so that no one could snatch even a smidgen of the grease from them.

t_ch: 你是说有人背后搞你？
t_en: Do you mean someone sabotaged you?

t_ch: 没错！虽然才入职没多久，但我已经敏锐地发现了匹诺康尼潜藏的商机。于是我经过缜密调查与分析，好不容易设计出了一份精彩绝伦的活动企划。
t_en: Bingo! Although I was new to the company at that time, I was sharp enough to discover the potential business opportunities in Penacony. After conducting a thorough investigation and analysis, I finally came up with an incredible event proposal.

t_ch: 我怀着满腔热忱将这份企划递交给费曼——我那个苛刻又无能的领导——可他却将我的企划批判得一文不值。
t_en: Bursting with confidence, I handed the proposal to Feinman, my demanding yet incompetent boss, only to receive harsh criticisms from him.

t_ch: 面对这种情况，我…我当然心有不甘，要知道，就连学校里最严厉的教授都没这么骂过我！
t_en: Needless to say, I... I was upset. Even the strictest professor in my college had never reprimanded me like that!

t_ch: 所以我决定自己偷偷加班完成这份企划，以证明自己的价值，让他对我刮目相看！
t_en: So, I decided to stay back after work to secretly work on the project. I wanted to prove my worth and impress Feinman!

t_ch: 我招募了一个*志同道合的伙伴*，辛辛苦苦几番奔走经营，可眼见都要成功了……
t_en: I enlisted the help of a "like-minded partner" and worked like a dog for the project. And just as we were about to succeed...

t_ch: 费曼却突然半路杀出，叫停了我的项目不说，还当着所有人的面将我狠狠斥责了一番。
t_en: Feinman showed up from nowhere! Not only did he call a halt to my project, but he even ripped me to shreds in front of everyone.

t_ch: 那之后…原本还算友好的同事们也渐渐开始疏远我，还时不时在我背后指指点点。
t_en: From then on... my co-workers, who used to be friendly, started distancing themselves from me. Occasionally, they would even point fingers behind my back.

t_ch: 后来我几番打听，才知道原来是费曼将黑锅甩给了我，说那段时间大家强制加班是因为我那「愚蠢的企划」得罪了家族……
t_en: I later asked around and found out that Feinman had made me his scapegoat. He claimed that the reason everyone had to work overtime during that period was because The Family wasn't happy with my "stupid project."

t_ch: 而之后他更是趁此机会，和苜蓿草家系的人频频接触，好像还真打算搞出什么名堂来。
t_en: After that, he took advantage of the situation to make frequent contact with the Alfalfa Family, seemingly brewing a huge plan.

t_ch: 可笑我当时竟真中了他的圈套，满心愧疚与惶恐无从宣泄，只好借苏乐达……
t_en: Foolishly, I fell for his trick at that time. As I had no other means to vent my guilt and fear, I sought solace in SoulGlad...

t_ch: 但如今看来，这人根本就是想通过打压、污蔑我，好将我的成果据为己有嘛！
t_en: It's now clear to me that he was taking credit for my work by oppressing and throwing mud at me!

t_ch: …你们大人的世界可真黑暗。
t_en: ...The world of adults sure is dark.

t_ch: 可恶！既然这样我也不能坐以待毙！得想出个对策才行…对策…对策…有了，这不是现成的嘛——
t_en: Drat! I can't sit around and do nothing! I need a plan... A plan... A plan... Wait a minute... Since you're here...

t_ch: 哎，您先别急着拒绝，我也不求您为我打抱不平、惩奸除恶。我就是想请您找我这个领导聊聊天。
t_en: Hey, don't turn me down so soon. I don't expect you to bring the bad guy to justice for me. All you need to do is chat with Feinman.

t_ch: 您瞧，您不是公司的人，他也必定*不知道我们俩的关系*。您就帮忙收集些证据，好让我能够揭露他的险恶阴谋、狡诈嘴脸！
t_en: Look, since you don't work at IPC, I'm certain Feinman isn't aware of our relationship. I need your help to gather evidence so that I can expose his true colors!

t_ch: 我就当您答应了啊——这样，您姑且先从<color=#f29e38ff>费曼身边</color>入手，找找能和他聊起来的*话题*吧。
t_en: I'll take that as a yes, then. Now, think about <color=#f29e38ff>things about Feinman</color> and find "topics" you can talk about to him.

t_ch: 还有这个，给您——我相信您的人品，所以也放心将我宝贵的企划书暂时交给您。希望您能将它用在需要的地方。
t_en: Also, this is for you. I have enough trust in you to let you keep my precious proposal for a while. I hope you will put it to good use.

t_ch: 他们现在应该就在【艾迪恩公园的吧台】。快去吧，我等您好消息！
t_en: You should be able to find them at the bar of Aideen Park. Go on! I'll wait for your good news.

t_ch: 您姑且先从<color=#f29e38ff>费曼身边</color>入手，找找能和他聊起来的*话题*吧。
t_en: Now, think about <color=#f29e38ff>things about Feinman</color> and find "topics" you can talk about to him.

t_ch: 他们现在应该就在【艾迪恩公园的吧台】。快去吧，我等您好消息！
t_en: You should be able to find them at the bar of Aideen Park. Go on! I'll wait for your good news.

t_ch: 哎，最近加班加得都快累死了，可算能好好休息一下了。
t_en: Alas, I've been exhausted from working overtime lately. I could really do with a break.

t_ch: 可不是！要是自愿为公司发光发热也就罢了，但浪费时间给一个傻子擦屁股？饶了我吧还是。
t_en: I couldn't agree more! It would be fine if I did it on my own accord, but cleaning up the mess for a fool? It's a straight no.

t_ch: 话也别说得那么难听嘛…谁还没个年轻气盛的时候呢，说不定人家已经在反省了。
t_en: Cut that poor guy some slack... We've all been young before, right? He's probably learned from his mistake already.

t_ch: 就他？得了吧，你看他盯着费曼先生的眼神，就跟看仇人似的。
t_en: Are you sure? Come on. Look at the way he stares at Mr. Feinman — it's like Mr. Feinman is his arch-enemy.

t_ch: 但愿那家伙能安分点。瞧他先前搞出的乱子，刚刚我路过吧台前的时候，看到那边的*电视*还在播报呢……
t_en: I hope that guy will watch his behavior. When I passed by the front of the counter earlier, the broadcaster happened to be talking about the mess he caused on "TV"...

t_ch: …电视播报？我也许该<color=#f29e38ff>去吧台前找台电视</color>看看……
t_en: ...On TV? I should probably <color=#f29e38ff>head to the front of the counter and find a TV</color> to watch...

t_ch: …近日，在「黄金的时刻」发生了多起诈骗事件，犯罪嫌疑人以老年游客为目标群体，诱骗受害者高价购买劣质珠宝。
t_en: ...Recently, there have been multiple cases of fraud in Golden Hour. The suspects targeted elderly tourists and deceived the victims into purchasing low-quality jewelry at exorbitant prices.

t_ch: 「猎犬」家系针对此事展开了全面而迅速的调查，并在有关人员的积极配合下，顺利将嫌犯逮捕归案。
t_en: The Bloodhound Family has conducted a thorough and prompt investigation into the matter and arrested the suspects with the help of relevant personnel.

t_ch: 在调查人员的不懈努力下，大部分赃款现已成功追回。
t_en: Thanks to the persistent effort of the investigation team, most of the stolen money has been recovered.

t_ch: 家族承诺将进一步加强治安管理，全面保障宾客们的人身与财产安全……
t_en: The Family pledged to further strengthen the security management to ensure the safety of the guests.

t_ch: …诈骗？这和波奇有什么关系？
t_en: ...Fraud? How are the cases related to Bocchi?

t_ch: 算了，调查得差不多了，准备去<color=#f29e38ff>和费曼聊聊</color>吧。
t_en: That's enough sleuthing for today. I'm going to <color=#f29e38ff>talk to Feinman</color>.

t_ch: 在开始之前，先看看我现在有什么可以谈论的*话题*吧。
t_en: Before I start, let's see what "topics" I can talk about.

t_ch: 员工的闲聊？
t_en: Employees' chitchat?

t_ch: 波奇的同事似乎因为加班而对他颇有怨言。这件事似乎和费曼脱不开关系。
t_en: Bocchi's co-workers seem to have a grudge against him because they are forced to work overtime. Feinman seems to have something to do with this.

t_ch: 电视新闻？
t_en: The TV news?

t_ch: 一则新闻报道，内容大致为某诈骗犯落网。虽然不知道此事和波奇有什么关系，总之先记下吧。
t_en: A news article about a swindler being caught. I'm writing this down, although I don't know how Bocchi can be related to the case.

t_ch: 《圣甲虫计划》？
t_en: Project: Scarab?

t_ch: 波奇的「宝贵的企划书」，不知道有没有机会用上。
t_en: Bocchi's "Precious Proposal." You're uncertain whether it'll see any use.

t_ch: 我已经全都确认过一遍了。
t_en: I've gone through everything.

t_ch: 好，准备上了！
t_en: Ready, go!

t_ch: …嗯，有事？
t_en: ...Um, is there anything I can do for you?

t_ch: 一个人吗，一起喝一杯？
t_en: Are you here alone? How about grabbing a drink together?

t_ch: 你刚不是还在我那些同事旁边，鬼鬼祟祟偷听了好半天吗？
t_en: Weren't you eavesdropping on my colleagues' conversation just now?

t_ch: 我刚不小心听到你同事聊天……
t_en: I overheard something when your colleagues were talking just now...

t_ch: 不小心？哼……
t_en: Overheard? Hmph...

t_ch: 有话直说吧，别浪费时间。你想要什么？
t_en: Just cut the nonsense and stop wasting my time. What do you want from me?

t_ch: 呃…关于波奇……
t_en: Um... About Bocchi...

t_ch: 让我猜猜…他叫你来套我的话，找些「证据」，留到以后伺机报复我？
t_en: Let me guess... Did he send you here to trick me into saying things he can use against me in the future?

t_ch: 现在的年轻人，啧…行了，用扁桃体也能猜到他怎么编排我的。
t_en: Youngsters these days... Tsk... I already know how he frames me.

t_ch: 他是不是说我打压他，还联合其他人一起排挤他？
t_en: Did he tell you I treated him oppressively and ostracized him along with others?

t_ch: 实际上呢，大家对他有怨气是因为——那小子犯了事，我们所有人为了给他收拾烂摊子，连续加了一个礼拜的班。
t_en: The truth is, that dude made a mistake. We're mean to him because we had to work overtime for a week to clean up his mess.

t_ch: 他倒是能耐得很，竟然勾搭了个「搞艺术」的一起到处诈骗，就为了证明他之前被我打回去的那个企划有多「靠谱」……
t_en: Guess what he did next? He partnered with a shady guy and started scamming people wherever he went just to prove that his rejected proposal was viable...

t_ch: 但平心而论，他那企划…不能说是有理有据，只能说是狗屁不通。
t_en: Honestly, that proposal of his... It's just outright nonsense.

t_ch: 现在你们可以好好*聊聊*了。
t_en: Now, you guys can "talk."

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 你没有吗？刚刚那几个人可是说了你们关系不好呢。
t_en: Didn't you? Those guys over there told me that Bocchi and you weren't on good terms.

t_ch: 哦？你倒是说说，怎么个不好？
t_en: Oh? Tell me more.

t_ch: 说就说，我都记下来了。
t_en: Sure. I've jotted it all down.

t_ch: 出示
t_en: (Present evidence)

t_ch: 就这？这只能说明他单方面仇视我吧。
t_en: That's it? Those things just show that he's harboring a one-sided grudge against me.

t_ch: 可如果你没做什么坏事，他为什么要仇视你？
t_en: Why does he resent you if you haven't done anything bad?

t_ch: 哈…你怎么不问我为什么今天出门先迈左脚呢？
t_en: Ha... Why don't you ask me why I stepped out of my house with my left foot first this morning?

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 难道不是你为了抢夺波奇的劳动成果，故意抹黑他的吗？
t_en: Didn't you badmouth Bocchi so that you could take credit for his work?

t_ch: 我抹黑他？我是有多闲才会去干这种无聊的事打发时间？
t_en: Me? Badmouth him? Do you think I have time to do meaningless stuff like that?

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 诈骗？刚刚那则新闻说的不会就是……
t_en: Scam? That news program earlier...

t_ch: 你也看到了？正好，省得我再费口舌了。
t_en: Did you watch it? Thank goodness I don't have to repeat the whole story.

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 可波奇说你是「想通过打压、污蔑他，好将他的成果据为己有」。
t_en: But Bocchi said that you wanted to take credit for his work by oppressing and throwing mud at him.

t_ch: …啊？你认真的…不是，他认真的？
t_en: ...Huh? Are you serious... I mean... Is he serious?

t_ch: 他还说那是他「经过缜密调查与分析，好不容易设计出的一份精彩绝伦的活动企划」。
t_en: He also said that he came up with the incredible event proposal after conducting a thorough investigation and analysis.

t_ch: 嗤，我还说自己是奥斯瓦尔多·施耐德呢，你信吗？<color=#f29e38ff>至于他那份企划究竟有多么「精彩绝伦」，我倒是建议你自己弄来看看。</color>
t_en: Tsk. Would you believe me if I told you I were Oswaldo Schneider? <color=#f29e38ff>As for how "marvelously outstanding" his designs are, well, I suggest you take a look yourself once you get your hands on them.</color>

t_ch: 反正我把企划带来了，你自己看吧。
t_en: Anyway, I've brought his proposal here. You can take a look.

t_ch: 出示
t_en: (Present evidence)

t_ch: 嚯，又是这玩意…说实话，我真不想再看一遍这种只会让人浪费生命的垃圾。
t_en: Oof, this again... Honestly, I really don't want to waste any more time on this kind of meaningless garbage.

t_ch: 你要是还不信，不如就自己瞧瞧，他到底写了个什么破玩意吧。
t_en: If you still don't believe me, you can read that crappy proposal yourself.

t_ch: 我从不讨厌有想法、有野心的年轻人。不过这种又蠢又坏的……
t_en: I never make things difficult for smart and ambitious youngsters. But for a dumb and mean one like him...

t_ch: 这件事你不用再掺和了。这是我们公司内部的问题，回头我会好好处理的。
t_en: You can stay out of this matter. Since this is an internal issue of our company, I'll handle it.

t_ch: 出示（使用错误的情况）
t_en: Show (incorrect use)

t_ch: 你看看这个。
t_en: Take a look at this.

t_ch: 我没时间看这些无关紧要的东西。
t_en: I'm too busy to read useless stuff like this.

t_ch: 这么快就回来了？他说了什么，快告诉我！
t_en: That was quick! What did he say? Tell me everything!

t_ch: 呵，我就知道这个老东西肯定会说我坏话……
t_en: Hmph, I knew that old guy would talk bad about me...

t_ch: 可惜这次没能抓到他的尾巴，但没关系，我会好好盯紧他的！
t_en: It's a pity we can't glean anything against him this time. Never mind. I'll keep watching him!

t_ch: …所以就是说，为什么要针对我啊！啊？为什么啊！
t_en: ...So, why does he only treat me this way? Why!?

t_ch: 我刚刚看到新闻了，我那个好伙伴…竟然被抓了，还被扣上了「诈骗犯」的帽子！
t_en: I saw the news just now. My partner... was arrested and labeled a fraudster!

t_ch: 这、这一定是费曼搞的鬼！怪不得他最近总和家族的人往来，他们一定是串通好的！
t_en: Th—This must be Feinman's doing! No wonder he's been contacting The Family a lot these days. They must be in cahoots!

t_ch: 我们不过就是顺应市场需求做了些小生意…真是的，一个两个，整天盯着我使绊子是什么意思？不行，我一定得想办法把人捞出来！
t_en: We're just catering to market demands and doing some small business... Seriously, why are they always targeting us? I have to find a way to get rid of them!

t_ch: 真是的，一个两个，整天盯着我使绊子是什么意思！嫉妒我能干、敢干是吧？
t_en: What's wrong with these people? Seems like they just can't wait to see me mess up! Are they jealous of my talent?

t_ch: 不行，我一定得想办法把人救出来！
t_en: I must think of a way to get my partner out!

t_ch: …嗯，企划书？这玩意你还留着呢？之前给你的只是份副本，不用还给我了。
t_en: ...My proposal? I'm surprised you still have it. It's just a copy, so you don't have to give it back to me.

t_ch: 我看到新闻了，我那个好伙伴…竟然被抓了，还被扣上了「诈骗犯」的帽子！
t_en: I saw the news just now. My partner... was arrested and labeled a fraudster!

t_ch: 这、这一定是费曼搞的鬼！怪不得他最近总和家族的人往来，他们一定是串通好的！
t_en: Th—This must be Feinman's doing! No wonder he's been contacting The Family a lot these days. They must be in cahoots!

t_ch: 我们不过就是顺应市场需求做了些小生意，不过就是为客户提供了些优质便捷的服务……
t_en: We were merely doing a small business as per market demands. It's about providing high-quality and convenient services for our clients.

t_ch: 真是的，一个两个，整天盯着我使绊子是什么意思！嫉妒我能干、敢干是吧？
t_en: What's wrong with these people? Seems like they just can't wait to see me mess up! Are they jealous of my talent?

t_ch: 不行，我一定得想办法把人救出来！
t_en: I must think of a way to get my partner out!

t_ch: 你发现波奇对待你的态度似乎比之前随意了许多。
t_en: You notice that Bocchi's attitude toward you is much less uptight than before.

t_ch: …没关系，即便这次被他抢了功劳，凭我的聪明才智，一定还能想出更好的企划。
t_en: ...Yes, he takes the credit this time, but I'm going to let it go. As brilliant as I am, I'm sure I can come up with a better project.

t_ch: 不过这也不是长久之计，得找个机会自立门户才行。
t_en: But staying here isn't the way to go. I must try to set up my own enterprise as soon as possible.

t_ch: 那群傻*同事也是，跟着这种嫉贤妒能、邀功诿过的上司，能有什么出路……
t_en: I don't understand why my co-workers are still sticking with a boss who's jealous of talented people and takes credit for everything. What kind of future do they expect when they work for Feinman...

t_ch: 一个个明里嫌弃，暗中排挤；不需要时好为人师，真需要时事不关己。
t_en: They all openly look down on me and secretly ostracize me. When their interests aren't at stake, they'll be quick to give advice. Otherwise, they'll just let things drift.

t_ch: 要是变得像他们一样，那还不如干脆把人生交给苏乐达呢！
t_en: I'd rather live my life drinking SoulGlad than become someone like them!

t_ch: …哦，对啦，您辛辛苦苦替我跑了一趟，我理应给您报酬的。
t_en: ...Oh, by the way, I should reward you for your help.

t_ch: …没关系，即便这次被他抢了功劳，凭我的聪明才智，一定还能想出更好的企划。
t_en: ...Yes, he takes the credit this time, but I'm going to let it go. As brilliant as I am, I'm sure I can come up with a better project.

t_ch: 不过这也不是长久之计，得找个机会自立门户才行。
t_en: But staying here isn't the way to go. I must try to set up my own enterprise as soon as possible.

t_ch: 那群傻*同事也是，跟着这种嫉贤妒能、邀功诿过的上司，能有什么出路……
t_en: I don't understand why my co-workers are still sticking with a boss who's jealous of talented people and takes credit for everything. What kind of future do they expect when they work for Feinman...

t_ch: 一个个明里嫌弃，暗中排挤；不需要时好为人师，真需要时事不关己。
t_en: They all openly look down on me and secretly ostracize me. When their interests aren't at stake, they'll be quick to give advice. Otherwise, they'll just let things drift.

t_ch: 要是变得像他们一样，那还不如干脆把人生交给苏乐达呢！
t_en: I'd rather live my life drinking SoulGlad than become someone like them!

t_ch: 我不明白……
t_en: I don't get it...

t_ch: 您说，我究竟哪里做得不够好呢…是因为我说话太直了吗？是因为我不会讨好人吗？
t_en: You say, where exactly am I falling short... Is it because I speak too frankly? Is it because I'm not a people-pleaser?

t_ch: 他们都说…像我这种初出茅庐的「菜鸟」，总得多听、多看、多求教，才能成熟稳重、少走弯路……
t_en: They say... a rookie like me should listen, observe, and ask questions more to gain experience and avoid unnecessary mistakes...

t_ch: 但成熟可不意味着每天浑水摸鱼虚度光阴，稳重也不代表要胸无大志心如死水！
t_en: But being senior doesn't mean you can start idling away your time and losing the drive to aim high!

t_ch: 他们叫我「空想家」、叫我「麻烦精」、叫我「坏事的」……
t_en: They call me "delusional," a "troublemaker," or "a loose cannon"...

t_ch: 可要我说，就算是「空想」，好歹也还能想、敢想；就算是「坏事」，好歹也还是做了事。
t_en: But then again, being "delusional" means I still have the ability and courage to dream. Even if I'm really a troublemaker, it's only because I'm trying to achieve something.

t_ch: 反倒是那些专精偷奸耍滑的市侩，和擅长冥顽不化的古董…哈哈！
t_en: The joke is on those mercenaries who cut corners and are too set in their ways to change! Haha!

t_ch: 说什么「一天到晚总给他们添麻烦」，不过是想打压刚入行的新人罢了。
t_en: They call me their burden because they want to bully me, a newbie.

t_ch: 我的热血与激情在熊熊燃烧——
t_en: The fire of passion is still burning inside me...

t_ch: 您知道吗…那段时间，我就像在暴雨中艰难行走，感官与外界彻底隔绝。我几乎忘记了理想的芬芳、热情的滚烫、尽情挥洒汗水后获得的甘甜……
t_en: Did you know that... There was a time when I felt as though I was slugging my way through torrential rain, and my senses were isolated from the outside world. I became oblivious to the enticing scent of ideals, the burning sensation of passion, and the sweet aftertaste of hard work...

t_ch: 这些游戏币给您！我决定抓紧人生中的每一秒，不能再浪费时间在玩乐上了！
t_en: These tokens are for you! I've decided to use every second of my life well. I can't waste any more time on fun!

t_ch: 但现在我已重新想起——只要热血和激情还未冷却，梦想就还有实现的可能！
t_en: But now, it's all come back to me... As long as my passion and motivation still burn bright, I still have a chance to achieve my dream!

t_ch: 如果只因前路泥泞艰辛就熄灭了灵魂的火焰，我和那些嘲笑我的人又有何区别！
t_en: If I lose my heart just because the road ahead is muddy and challenging, I'll be no different from those who mock me!

t_ch: 我向苏萨先生、奥斯瓦尔多主管、以及之前数以百计的偶像们起誓——
t_en: Now, I swear to Mr. Sousa, Oswaldo, and those many idols I've previously had...

t_ch: ——未来的某天，我一定要点燃心底的烈火！哪怕烧尽自己，也要让梦想大放光彩！
t_en: That I'll one day ignite the ferocious fire of my heart! I'll illuminate my dream, even if I burn myself to embers!

t_ch: 我还真把企划带来了……
t_en: I've actually brought the plan...

t_ch: 我这人最讨厌无效加班……
t_en: Well, I hate pointless overtime...

t_ch: 你将刚刚发生的事情一五一十地告知波奇。
t_en: You tell Bocchi what truly happened.
Mmm...

t_ch: 嗯…嗯嗯……
t_en: Hmm... hmmm...

t_ch: 你在干嘛？
t_en: What are you doing?

t_ch: 丢东西了？
t_en: Have you lost something?

t_ch: …嗯？别打岔！我正在认真思考商业化战略，你连这点眼力见都没有嘛？
t_en: ...Hmm? Don't distract me! I'm in the middle of a serious commercialization strategy!

t_ch: 嗬，你这人，身上穿的这叫啥…？有点意思，我在黄金的时刻还很少看到衣品这么…朴素的人。哦，为了体验生活刻意扮惨的家伙除外。
t_en: Heh, look at you! What's that you're wearing...? Funny, I've rarely seen anyone so... "plainly dressed" in Golden Hour. Oh, well, except for those guys deliberately trying to look miserable.

t_ch: …噢，有了！多亏了你突然出现，我这就想到一个绝妙的点子！
t_en: ...Oh, yes! Thanks to your sudden appearance, I just came up with a brilliant idea!

t_ch: 点子的名字就叫「乞丐角色扮演」——乖乖，不愧是我！这想法绝对能火！
t_en: It's called "Beggar's Life Simulation"! Boy, this idea will be a hit!

t_ch: 至于那么寒酸吗？！
t_en: Do I really look that bad?

t_ch: 长双眼睛吧，{F#小姐}{M#小哥}！好好看看周围的人都是怎么打扮自己的，你在这座城市里就是个妥妥的异类！
t_en: Come on, {F#miss}{M#mister}! Take a good look at how everyone around you dresses up. You stick out like a sore thumb in this city!

t_ch: 乞丐也是有尊严的。
t_en: Even beggars have dignity.

t_ch: 听你这话，经验丰富啊？
t_en: Oh, speaking from experience, huh?

t_ch: 看看你的周围吧，{F#小姐}{M#小哥}！看看周围的人都是怎么打扮自己的，你在这座城市里就是个妥妥的异类！
t_en: Just take a good look around, {F#miss}{M#mister}, and see how everyone around you dresses up! You stick out like a sore thumb in this city!

t_ch: 但是，朋友，别怕——特立独行在这地方可是不可多得的品质。在物欲横飞的世界里，坚持做自己可没那么容易！
t_en: That being said, don't be afraid, my friend! Being a maverick is a rare quality in this part of the world. I know, it's not easy to stay true to yourself!

t_ch: 所以你就坚持自己的道路，继续当个谁都懒得多看一眼的穷酸{F#姑娘}{M#小子}吧？我向你保证，这样所有人都能收获最大程度的乐子。
t_en: Why don't you stick to your own path and continue to be a poor {F#lass}{M#lad} that no one cares about? I promise you, That would be the most fun for everyone.

t_ch: 你是哪位，我和你很熟吗？
t_en: Who are you? Have we met before?

t_ch: 我？我叫艾默里奇，是个发明家。
t_en: Me? I'm Emmrich, an inventor.

t_ch: 你说话我可真不爱听。
t_en: I don't like the way you talk to me.

t_ch: 怎么，被事实猛敲一棍就遭不住了？
t_en: I see. Can't handle the truth, huh?

t_ch: 你到底是哪位？
t_en: Who on earth are you?

t_ch: 你最好记住我的名字，{F#姑娘}{M#小子}…我叫艾默里奇，是个发明家。
t_en: Well, you'd better remember my name, {F#lass}{M#lad}... I'm Emmrich, an inventor.

t_ch: 不是那种缩在小屋里瞎捣鼓的穷酸发明家——你在这座城里见证的所有奇观，里面起码有三分之一都该被打上我的专利！
t_en: I'm not one of those third-rate inventors who tinker in their huts. No. At least a third of all the wonders you're seeing in this city were invented by yours truly!

t_ch: 可惜呀，可惜…怪我刚来这梦境都市的时候缺少维权意识，让别人把我的好点子全都偷了去。
t_en: But what a shame... I blame my lack of awareness of rights when I first came here, letting others steal all my splendid ideas.

t_ch: 过来人的建议：要是哪天你脑子里突然冒出来个好想法，别急着告诉别人…提前想想它潜在的商业价值，别让那些脑袋空空、只会耍心眼的混蛋把你的果子摘走。
t_en: A word to the wise: If you have a great idea that pops into your head one day, don't be in a hurry to tell anyone about it... Think about its potential business value, so those empty-headed scoundrels won't take it away from you.

t_ch: 我还有事要办，没空闲聊…最后再听我一句劝，随便去哪个店里整件漂亮衣裳吧。多少给自己留点体面，行吗？
t_en: I've got things to do, so I don't have time for idle chatter... Just go into any store and get yourself a decent outfit, will you?

t_ch: 还想和你聊两句。
t_en: I'd like to have a chat with you.

t_ch: 嗯？找我有事？
t_en: Huh? Do you have questions?

t_ch: 你都发明了什么？
t_en: What have you invented?

t_ch: 会飞的高级跑车、能自动给人烫头的街头造型师机器人、一口能尝出十七种味道的超级复合食物添加剂——
t_en: Flying sports cars, street stylist robots, a super-complex food additive that adds seventeen flavors in one bite...

t_ch: ——还要我一个个数过去吗？不好意思，没空！
t_en: ...Do you want me to list them all? I'm sorry, but I don't have time for that!

t_ch: 我不太喜欢你的态度。
t_en: I'm not particularly fond of your attitude.

t_ch: 「真对不起，{F#小姐}{M#先生}！我以名誉保证，我的玩笑里没有恶意…我真的没想到会伤及您的感情，请您接受我最郑重的道歉……」
t_en: "Oh, I'm truly sorry, {F#Ms.}{M#Mr.}! I sincerely assure you there was no ill intent in my joke... and I genuinely didn't intend to hurt your feelings. So, please accept my most sincere apology..."

t_ch: …想让我用这种恶心的腔调和每个路过的白痴说话？见鬼去吧，想都别想！
t_en: ...You want me to talk to every passing fool in that nauseating accent? Hell no! Don't even think about it!

t_ch: 你很需要钱？
t_en: Are you in urgent need of money?

t_ch: 钱？怎么，我看着像缺钱的人吗？
t_en: Money? What the... Do I look like I need money?

t_ch: 别开玩笑了，我靠卖专利赚的钱，普通人八辈子也花不完！
t_en: Don't be ridiculous. I've made more money from selling patents than average people could spend in eight lifetimes!

t_ch: 那你还那么在乎专利干嘛？
t_en: Then why do you care so much about your patents?

t_ch: 很简单——因为那些本就是我的想法，我的创意，我的设计！
t_en: It's simple! Because they are my ideas, my creations, and my designs!

t_ch: 这事压根和钱无关，它关乎公正、规矩、还有…还有名声，懂吗？
t_en: It's not about the money. It's about justice, rules, and... reputation, you know?

t_ch: 皮皮西人的四肢跃动起来。你从他的肢体动作中读出了热情、欢畅，还有些…狂妄。
t_en: The Pepeshi's limbs leap up. You read enthusiasm, jubilation, and some... hubris in his body movements.

t_ch: …哈？怎么回事？我感觉…我突然感觉棒极了！
t_en: ...Huh? What's going on? I... I suddenly feel wonderful!

t_ch: 我活过来了…这下彻底精神了！有两百个伟大的点子突然涌进了我的脑海——全宇宙的蠢货得在伟大的艾默里奇面前下跪！
t_en: I'm alive... and fully revitalized! Two hundred great ideas just flooded into my mind! All the fools will kneel down before the great Emmrich!

t_ch: 让博识学会的庸才全都见鬼去吧！满脑子「方法论」和「产品观」的家伙，根本创造不出有灵魂的发明！
t_en: To hell with the mediocrity of the Intelligentsia Guild! Those guys who know nothing other than so-called "methodology" and "product ideas" will never create anything with a soul!

t_ch: 早晚有一天，我——伟大的艾默里奇——的发明会冲出梦境，从公司手里抢走一块蛋糕！
t_en: One day, I, the Great Emmrich, will come up with groundbreaking inventions and steal a piece of the pie from the IPC!

t_ch: 现在是幻想时间…
t_en: Guess it's fantasy time...

t_ch: 耶！真棒！哇哦！
t_en: Yay! Awesome! Wow!

t_ch: 你——对，别东张西望了，说的就是你！我现在需要你帮忙！
t_en: Hey, you! Yeah, stop lollygagging! I need your help, now!

t_ch: 你该感到荣幸才对，可不是人人都有机会在老艾默里奇面前表现自己。
t_en: You should be honored. Not everyone gets a chance to impress ol' Emmrich.

t_ch: 来吧，我给你看一样东西……
t_en: Come. Let me show you something...

t_ch: 这是什么？
t_en: Huh. What's this?

t_ch: 不就是一张纸吗？
t_en: It's just a piece of paper, right?

t_ch: 你以为这就是简简单单的一张纸？大错特错！
t_en: You think it's "just" a piece of paper? Hah, dead wrong!

t_ch: 告诉你，它可能是匹诺康尼历史上最伟大的发明之一！仔细听好了，我给它取的名字——「长效专利捍卫一纸通」！
t_en: Behold, this could be one of the greatest inventions in the history of Penacony! Listen carefully, it's called the "Indefinite Patent Operative Omnifier."

t_ch: 什么乱七八糟的…
t_en: What the heck are you harping on about...

t_ch: 哼，怪不得人家说高个子种族没有慧根呢……
t_en: Heh, no wonder they say tall folks lack wisdom...

t_ch: 简称「肠胃通」？
t_en: So, abbreviated as "I-POO"?

t_ch: …警告一次！对我的发明放尊重点！
t_en: ...Consider yourself warned! Show some respect for my invention!

t_ch: 只要稍微关注过一点新闻就应该知道，在现实世界里，放眼四海皆是围绕知识产权或者技术专利产生的纠纷。
t_en: If you've been keeping up with the news, you know there's always some drama over intellectual property or patents in the real world.

t_ch: 哪里出现了文明，哪里就会出现侵权行为…这甚至不能算法律知识了，把它当成客观存在的物理法则都完全合理！
t_en: Wherever there's civilization, there's infringement... It's not just legal stuff anymore... It's practically a law of physics!

t_ch: …但是凭着这样发明，我将在匹诺康尼内部彻底杜绝一切侵权行为。等着瞧吧，历史会证明，「长效专利捍卫一纸通」的问世是象征宇宙文明跃进的一大步！
t_en: ...But with this invention, I'll wipe out all infringement in Penacony. Trust me, history will look back on this Indefinite Patent Operative Omnifier as a milestone in universal civilization!

t_ch: 更难能可贵的是，它的使用方法特别简便…看好了！
t_en: And the best part? It's super easy to use... Watch this!

t_ch: 艾默里奇小手一挥，将「长效专利捍卫一纸通」贴在了身边的墙上……
t_en: Emmrich waves his hand and sticks the Indefinite Patent Operative Omnifier on the wall beside him...

t_ch: 咻咻…正在进行侵权风险分析……
t_en: *Beep*... Initiating infringement risk analysis...

t_ch: 咻咻…分析完成！本次分析对象：「通用匹诺康尼建筑墙面」。
t_en: Beep... Analysis complete! Object identified: Generic architectural wall-covering in Penacony.

t_ch: 咻咻…结论：该分析对象所涉及之原材料、供应链及相关技术无侵权风险，可以正常投入应用情境。
t_en: Beep... Conclusion: The analyses regarding the raw materials, supply chain, and related technologies show no risk of infringement. This object can be used without risk.

t_ch: 看见没？这就是「长效专利捍卫一纸通」的厉害！有了它，我保准能赚上一大笔…啊不是，保准能保护匹诺康尼所有良心创造者的权益不受侵害！
t_en: See? That's the power of the Indefinite Patent Operative Omnifier! With this, I'm gonna make a boatload of money... Ahem, I mean... I'm gonna protect the rights of all talented inventors in Penacony from infringement!

t_ch: 来，拿几张走，看到什么值…咳，看到什么上档次的玩意就贴一张上去！记住了，一切都是为了社会的公正和进步！
t_en: Here, take a couple and go. Slap them on anything that's worth... Ahem, anything that looks important! Remember, it's all about social justice and civilization!

t_ch: 你得给我指定几样东西…
t_en: But you've got to give me some more details...

t_ch: 唔…确实不能让能力有所欠缺的高个子下决断。行吧，那我就给你指定几个「分析对象」……
t_en: Fine... I guess a tall fella with limited abilities just can't handle making decisions. Alright, I'll give you a few objects to analyze...

t_ch: 对我有什么好处？
t_en: What's in it for me?

t_ch: 贪心的高个子！能获得伟大的艾默里奇的赏识，这难道还不够吗？
t_en: You greedy tall folk! Isn't it enough to be recognized by the Great Emmrich?

t_ch: …行吧，你放心！只要帮我这个忙，好处绝对少不了你的！
t_en: ...Fine, alright! Just do me this favor, and I'll make it worth your while!

t_ch: 唔…转念一想，最好还是帮你缩小下分析对象的范围，免得你浪费这么珍贵的发明……
t_en: Well... On second thought... I should narrow down the scope of the objects you're to analyze so we won't waste this great invention...

t_ch: …好，决定了！<color=#dbc291ff>「斯坦威游隼」豪华飞车、观景望远镜、还有「推销员」广告牌</color>——你就先去分析这几样东西吧。
t_en: ...Okay, it's settled! <color=#dbc291ff>Go analyze a "Steinway Falcon" speedster, a viewing telescope, and a "sales" billboard</color> for now.

t_ch: 真奇怪，我手上有这么厉害的发明，之前怎么没想着用呢…算了，想那么多干嘛？现在动手也不算晚！
t_en: Weird... I have such an awesome invention, yet I didn't think of using it before... But never mind! It's better late than never!

t_ch: 「长效专利捍卫一纸通」的用法简单直接，看到什么可疑的玩意，把它往上面一拍就行了！
t_en: Using the Indefinite Patent Operative Omnifier is simple! Whenever you spot something suspicious, just slap the Omnifier on it!

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「斯坦威游隼」豪华飞车、观景望远镜、还有「推销员」广告牌</color>——你就先去分析这几样东西吧！记得，拿到分析报告以后速速向我汇报！
t_en: Go analyze a <color=#dbc291ff>"Steinway Falcon" luxury speedster, a viewing telescope, and a "sales" billboard</color>! Remember, report back to me as soon as you have the analysis results!

t_ch: 真奇怪，我手上有这么厉害的发明，之前怎么没想着用呢…算了，想那么多干嘛？现在动手也不算晚！
t_en: Weird... I have such an awesome invention, yet I didn't think of using it before... But never mind! It's better late than never!

t_ch: 斯坦威游隼，加长型豪华飞车。运行平稳，乘坐舒适……
t_en: Steinway Falcon, extended luxury speedster: Soar in Style, Embrace Luxury...

t_ch: …之类之类的，随便它吧。你不是来听广告词的，赶紧完成艾默里奇交给你的任务吧。
t_en: ...Well, who cares about the jingles? Just get the job done and finish the task Emmrich gave you.

t_ch: 「啪」——你将「长效专利捍卫一纸通」贴到了飞车上，熟练得像是曾经沿街发过十年的小广告。
t_en: With a "slap," you skillfully stick the Indefinite Patent Operative Omnifier onto the vehicle, as if you've been handing out flyers on the street for a decade.

t_ch: 咻咻…正在进行侵权风险分析……
t_en: *Beep*... Initiating infringement risk analysis...

t_ch: 咻咻…分析完成！本次分析对象：「斯坦威游隼」豪华飞车，型号：XV8。
t_en: *Beep*... Analysis complete! Object identified: Steinway Falcon luxury speedster, Model XV8.

t_ch: 咻咻…结论：包括车身、底盘、纵梁、内饰、发动机等<unbreak>98.7%</unbreak>的结构均无专利侵权风险。
t_en: *Beep*... Conclusion: <unbreak>98.7%</unbreak> of the structure, including the main body, chassis, beam, interior, engine, and other parts, is free from patent infringement risk.

t_ch: 咻咻…目前存在的风险：车前灯采用的配光镜使用了艾默里奇先生独创的超低能耗散光技术。该技术的商用规范目前暂不明确。
t_en: *Beep*... Current risk: The light distribution lenses in the car's headlights adopt Mr. Emmrich's exclusive ultra-low-energy diffraction technology. The commercial specifications for this technology are unclear at the moment.

t_ch: 咻咻…为保障一切专利创造者的合法权益，现在将对「斯坦威游隼」豪华飞车施行强制锁定操作。锁定将在现存的风险彻底清除后自动解除！
t_en: *Beep*... To protect the rights of patent creators, a mandatory lock will now be put on your "Steinway Falcon" luxury speedster. Please don't worry. The lock will be automatically unlocked once the infringement risk is resolved!

t_ch: 「肠胃通」的长篇大论结束了——你眼睁睁地看着面前的豪华飞车变成了一坨无法启动的废铁。
t_en: The long speech of the "I-POO" is finally over, and you watch a fancy luxury car in front of you turning into a useless pile of scrap metal.

t_ch: 你感觉心情有些复杂。或许你可以留下来看看车主的反应…或是直接转头离开。
t_en: You feel a mix of emotions. Maybe you should stay for a while to witness the owner's reaction... or just walk away.

t_ch: …怎么回事？刚才遥控器按了半天，怎么一点反应都没有？
t_en: What the...? I just pressed the button on my remote control, but why is my car not responding at all?

t_ch: 等会儿，这是什么？「长效专利捍卫一纸通」…什么乱七八糟的？
t_en: Hold on, what's this? Indefinite Patent Operative Omnifier... What kind of nonsense is this?

t_ch: 咻咻…车主您好。您驾驶的「斯坦威游隼」豪华飞车，其使用的配件存在专利侵权风险。
t_en: *Beep*... Greetings, vehicle owner. The accessories in your "Steinway Falcon" luxury speedster might be infringing on some patents.

t_ch: 咻咻…为保障一切专利创造者的合法权益，现在将对「斯坦威游隼」豪华飞车施行强制锁定操作。锁定将在现存的侵权风险彻底清除后自动解除！
t_en: *Beep*... To protect the rights of patent creators, a mandatory lock has been put on your "Steinway Falcon" luxury speedster. Please don't worry. The lock will be automatically unlocked once the infringement risk is resolved!

t_ch: 什、什么？！不对，你才没权力这么做！这是我的车，我花了大价钱买下来的！
t_en: Wh—What!? No way! This is my speedster! I paid a fortune for it!

t_ch: 不过是张破纸条，看我把你撕——
t_en: You're just a useless piece of paper! I'll just rip you...

t_ch: ——撕、撕不下来？！这到底是什么邪术？
t_en: Wh—What? I can't rip it up!? What kind of sorcery is this?

t_ch: 你、你不能这么对我！我答应了凯瑟琳，今天要带她在黄金的时刻兜风！你这是，你这是…啊啊啊啊啊啊！
t_en: No! Y—You can't do this to me! I promised Catherine that I'd take her for a ride in the Golden Hour today! You... you are... you... Aaarrrggghhh!

t_ch: 供游客欣赏梦境时刻壮丽景象的望远镜。居然连这种基础设施都不放过，真有你的啊艾默里奇。
t_en: A telescope for tourists to enjoy the breathtaking views of the Dreamscape. Emmrich doesn't even leave behind such infrastructure. What a total jerk.

t_ch: 赶紧完成他给你布置的任务，回去领取你违背良心换来的奖励吧。
t_en: Just get the task done already, so you can go back and grab the reward for selling your soul.

t_ch: 「啪」——你将「长效专利捍卫一纸通」贴到了望远镜上，心想这种东西不太可能侵权。
t_en: With a "slap," you stick the Indefinite Patent Operative Omnifier onto the telescope, thinking to yourself that it's unlikely to infringe on anything.

t_ch: 咻咻…正在进行侵权风险分析……
t_en: *Beep*... Initiating infringement risk analysis...

t_ch: 咻咻…分析完成！本次分析对象：「匹诺康尼观景望远镜」。
t_en: *Beep*... Analysis complete! Object identified: A viewing telescope in Penacony.

t_ch: 咻咻…结论：该分析对象所涉及之原材料、供应链及相关技术无侵权风险。
t_en: *Beep*... Conclusion: The analyses regarding the raw materials, supply chain, and related technologies show no risk of infringement.

t_ch: 咻咻…目前存在的风险：家族筑梦师曾承诺将望远镜设计为可自动调节高度，以满足不同身型宾客的需求。显然，当下的望远镜并未配置高度调节功能。
t_en: *Beep*... Current risk: The Dreamweavers of The Family have promised that the telescope would adjust its height to accommodate guests of different sizes. However, the current telescope does not have that feature.

t_ch: 咻咻…这一设计漏洞可能导致包括艾默里奇先生在内的皮皮西族宾客受到情感伤害，进而导致潜在的法理风险。
t_en: *Beep*... This design flaw could cause emotional distress to Pepeshi guests, including Mr. Emmrich himself, and potentially lead to legal trouble.

t_ch: 咻咻…为保障全体匹诺康尼宾客的合理权益，现在将对「匹诺康尼观景望远镜」施行强制锁定操作。锁定将在现存的风险彻底清除后自动解除！
t_en: *Beep*... To protect the rights of all guests in Penacony, a mandatory lock will now be put on the viewing telescope. The lock will be automatically unlocked once the infringement risk is resolved!

t_ch: 「肠胃通」的长篇大论结束了——眼前的望远镜也变成了瞎眼镜。
t_en: Alright, the long speech about the "I-POO" is finally over, and the telescope in front of you has turned into a useless piece of junk.

t_ch: 你感觉心情有些复杂。或许你可以留下来看看其他人的反应…或是直接转头离开。
t_en: You feel a mix of emotions. Maybe you should stay for a while to witness the others' reactions... or just walk away.

t_ch: 出问题的就是这只望远镜？
t_en: So, is this the telescope that went haywire?

t_ch: 明显是，没看见上面贴的纸条吗？「长效专利捍卫一纸通」…这又是什么新式整蛊？
t_en: Obviously. Didn't you see the note taped to it? Indefinite Patent Operative Omnifier?... What kind of new trickery is this?

t_ch: 最近的逐梦客，花活真是越来越多了…少添点乱，多享受享受生活不行吗？
t_en: Those Dreamchasers just won't stop causing trouble... Can't they just peacefully enjoy their life in this dream?

t_ch: 嘿——嘿呀——
t_en: I'll... rip it off...

t_ch: ——不行，撕不下来。看来不是一般的纸条，要不还是把这事汇报上去吧？
t_en: ...Hmm, I can't rip it off. It doesn't seem to be an ordinary note. Maybe we should report it?

t_ch: …这么点小事也要惊动家主？不至于吧……
t_en: ...You want to disturb the family head over such a small matter? Think again...

t_ch: 等这些纸条像牛皮癣一样出现在大街小巷里，到时候你就不这么想了。防患于未然，还是小心点好……
t_en: What will happen if these notes start appearing everywhere like a bad rash? It's better safe than sorry...

t_ch: 酥酥麻麻，一秒舒爽！
t_en: Zap away discomfort! Instant relief!

t_ch: 电电枪，最棒棒！
t_en: The best of the best: Zappy Gun!

t_ch: 酥酥麻麻，一秒舒爽！电电枪，最棒棒！
t_en: Zap away discomfort! Instant relief! The best of the best: Zappy Gun!

t_ch: 立马下单，还有机会赢取酥麻推拿套餐大礼包！
t_en: Order now and you might win the Zappy Zap-Up Bundle!

t_ch: 你完全搞不懂这所谓的「电电枪」是干嘛用的…听上去多少沾些不正经。
t_en: You have no idea what this "Zappy Gun" is supposed to do... although it does sound a bit questionable.

t_ch: 无所谓，你本来也没打算买这些没用的玩具。赶紧完成艾默里奇的任务吧。
t_en: But anyway, you're not planning on buying these weird toys anyway. Just finish Emmrich's task.

t_ch: 「啪」——你将「长效专利捍卫一纸通」贴到了广告牌上。广告牌应声倒下…但你确信自己根本没用那么大的力气。
t_en: With a "slap," you stick the Indefinite Patent Operative Omnifier onto the sales billboard. The billboard topples over, but you swear you didn't exert much force.

t_ch: 咻咻…正在进行侵权风险分析……
t_en: *Beep*... Initiating infringement risk analysis...

t_ch: 咻咻…分析完成！本次分析对象：「广告牌推销员」。
t_en: *Beep*... Analysis complete! Object identified: A sales billboard.

t_ch: 咻咻…结论：该分析对象所涉及之原材料、供应链及相关技术无侵权风险。
t_en: *Beep*... Conclusion: The analyses regarding the raw materials, supply chain, and related technologies show no risk of infringement.

t_ch: 咻咻…目前存在的风险：该广告牌所宣传的产品是艾默里奇先生的发明，且艾默里奇先生与其分销商就销售额分成一事仍存争论。
t_en: *Beep*... Current risk: The billboard is advertising Mr. Emmrich's invention, and he's currently in a dispute with his distributor over the sales share.

t_ch: 咻咻…为保障艾默里奇先生作为发明者的正当权益，现在将对「广告牌推销员」施行强制锁定操作。锁定将在现存的风险彻底清除后自动解除！
t_en: *Beep*... To ensure Mr. Emmrich's rights as the inventor, a mandatory lock will now be put on the sales billboard. The lock will be automatically unlocked once the infringement risk is resolved!

t_ch: 广告牌不再聒噪，你的内心也感觉空落落的——为了那个自私自利的大发明家，你居然下了如此狠手。
t_en: The billboard is not making sounds anymore, and it's making you feel bad. You can't believe you went to such lengths for that selfish inventor.

t_ch: 你感觉心情有些复杂。或许你可以留下来看看后续会发生什么…或是直接转头离开。
t_en: You feel a mix of emotions. Maybe you should stay for a while to witness others' reactions... or just walk away.

t_ch: 哦，{RUBY_B#「同谐」}神主{RUBY_E#}在上！我们的好兄弟怎么倒下了？！
t_en: Oh! {RUBY_B#Harmony}Great One{RUBY_E#}! How did our poor brother end up like this!

t_ch: 妙极！妙极！电电枪广告牌挂了，这下大家都会抢着来买钻钻娃了！
t_en: Wonderful! Now that the Zappy Gun billboard is done for, everyone will flock to buy the Diamond Doll!

t_ch: 伙计，你真是缺少同情心！我们的兄弟横尸街头，你居然能如此幸灾乐祸！
t_en: Seriously, mate? Have a little empathy! How can you be so heartless when our brother is lying dead on the street?

t_ch: 第一！我们是广告牌，哪来的心？
t_en: We're billboards, man! We don't have "empathy"!

t_ch: 第二！你当然站着说话不腰疼——它卖的又不是你的竞品，影响的又不是你的业绩！
t_en: And besides, that billboard was not promoting your competitor's product or messing with your business! Of course you can just play the good guy!

t_ch: 「长效专利捍卫一纸通」的用法简单直接，看到什么可疑的玩意，把它往上面一拍就行了！
t_en: Using the Indefinite Patent Operative Omnifier is simple! Whenever you spot something suspicious, just slap the Omnifier on it!

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「斯坦威游隼」豪华飞车、观景望远镜、还有「推销员」广告牌</color>——你就先去分析这几样东西吧！记得，拿到分析报告以后速速向我汇报！
t_en: Go analyze a <color=#dbc291ff>"Steinway Falcon" luxury speedster, a viewing telescope, and a "sales" billboard</color>! Remember, report back to me as soon as you have the analysis results!

t_ch: 真奇怪，我手上有这么厉害的发明，之前怎么没想着用呢…算了，想那么多干嘛？现在动手也不算晚！
t_en: Weird... I have such an awesome invention, yet I didn't think of using it before... But never mind! It's better late than never!

t_ch: 交给我的事已经办妥了。
t_en: I've done what you asked me to do.

t_ch: 看不出来啊，你这高个子手脚还怪麻利的！快，把过程给我讲讲！
t_en: Honestly, I didn't expect tall folks with such quick reflexes. Well, tell me how you did it!

t_ch: 把你到处张贴「长效专利捍卫一纸通」的事迹如实汇报给了艾默里奇……
t_en: You report to Emmrich how you put up those Indefinite Patent Operative Omnifier...
Ha...

t_ch: <size=36>哈哈哈哈哈哈！！！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Hahahahaha!!!</size>

t_ch: 眼前的皮皮西人突然爆发出癫狂的大笑…你无法相信他小小的身体里居然蕴藏了如此庞大的能量。
t_en: The Pepeshi in front of you suddenly bursts into maniacal laughter... You can't believe the amount of energy he possesses in his tiny body.

t_ch: 好，很好！你做得太好了，傻大…高个子{F#小姐}{M#先生}！
t_en: Great! Well done, gulli... I mean, tall {F#lady}{M#gentleman}!

t_ch: 我就知道，「长效专利捍卫一纸通」绝对是划时代的发明！有了它…只要有了它，我就能剑指黄金的时刻——不对，是剑指整个匹诺康尼的首富了！
t_en: I knew it! The Indefinite Patent Operative Omnifier is a game-changer! With it, I'll be qualified for Golden Hour... No, I'll become the richest guy in person in Penacony!

t_ch: 「为了公正和进步」哪去了？
t_en: But I thought it was all about "justice and progress"?

t_ch: 哦…那个啊，那个当然也很重要。当然，我可没忘！
t_en: Oh, right... that... Yeah, that's important too! I haven't forgotten it! Of course not!

t_ch: 你就是个财迷，知道吗？
t_en: You're all about the money, aren't you?

t_ch: 嗯？你说什么？
t_en: Hmm? What did you say?

t_ch: 哦对，想起来了，这也是为了宇宙商业规则的公正和进步！当然，我可没忘！
t_en: Oh yeah, it's also about justice and civilization in the galactic business world! I haven't forgotten it! Of course not!

t_ch: 等着瞧吧——属于我，伟大发明家艾默里奇的商业帝国正在冉冉升起！格拉克斯大道？迟早改姓！
t_en: Just you wait and see! I, Emmrich, the Great Inventor, will build a business empire in Penacony! Glaux Avenue? Hah, one day, it'll become Emmrich Avenue!

t_ch: 噢，想起来了！之前说好的，给你这次帮忙的报酬——拿去。
t_en: Oh, right! Here, the payment I promised. It's yours now.

t_ch: …怎么，嫌少？别拿那种眼神看我！能和匹诺康尼未来的首富搭上关系，你理应感恩戴德才对！
t_en: ...What, not enough? Don't give me that look! You should be grateful to be associated with the future richest man in Penacony!

t_ch: 在你眼前的可是匹诺康尼未来的首富，伟大的大发明家艾默里奇！
t_en: You're looking at the Great Inventor Emmrich, the future richest man in Penacony!

t_ch: 和我说话的态度就可能就决定了你未来的人生——说这句话的意思是，对我和我的发明都放尊重点！
t_en: The way you talk to me might determine your future, you know? Treat me and my inventions with respect!

t_ch: 艾默里奇摆出了那副标志性的得意神态——令人生厌，但却和他本人的形象非常适配。
t_en: Emmrich puts on his signature annoying smirk, which suits him well.

t_ch: 哼哼…有了「长效专利捍卫一纸通」，我就等于变相掌控了大半个匹诺康尼！
t_en: Haha... With the Indefinite Patent Operative Omnifier, I practically control the majority of Penacony!

t_ch: 谁叫家族就爱剽窃我的点子呢？出来混迟早要交专利费的，哈哈哈哈！
t_en: The Family loves stealing my ideas, right? Hah, they'll have to pay me royalties in the end! Hahaha!

t_ch: 眼前的皮皮西人掉进了妄想的漩涡。让你感到恐怖的是——他还真有可能成功。
t_en: This Pepeshi person is lost in his own delusions, and here's the scariest part: He might actually succeed.

t_ch: 唔…之前把观景望远镜给锁定了以后，立马就惊动了家族的人。
t_en: Well... When the telescope was locked, The Family got alerted right away.

t_ch: 后面做事还是得小心点。还是先拿逐梦客开刀比较好……
t_en: I should be more careful about my next step. Maybe it's better to target those Dreamchasers...

t_ch: 艾默里奇冷静了下来…皮皮西人眼里通常带着一种默认的贪婪，这一特质在他身上更为凸显，毫无掩饰。
t_en: Emmrich has calmed down... While the Pepeshi people are known for the unmistakable greed in their eyes, his is on a whole other level.

t_ch: 唔…之前把观景望远镜给锁定了以后，立马就惊动了家族的人。
t_en: Well... When the telescope was locked, The Family got alerted right away.

t_ch: 后面做事还是得小心点。还是先拿逐梦客开刀比较好…让我想想，除了豪华飞车，还有什么商品用到了我的专利……
t_en: I should be more careful about my next step. Maybe it's better to target those Dreamchasers... Let's see what other things have infringed on my patents...

t_ch: *皮皮西族粗口*特拉维斯那个叛徒！败类！皮皮西族之耻！
t_en: *Pepeshi curse* Travis! That traitor! Scumbag! A disgrace among us Pepeshi!

t_ch: 凡事都要阻挠我，装什么清高！发明家想靠自己的专利挣钱有错吗？啊？！
t_en: Why does he keep trying to mess with me, acting all high and mighty!? What's the big deal if an inventor wants to cash in on their patents, huh?

t_ch: 艾默里奇突然蹦了起来，捏紧的拳头差点锤到你的膝盖——好险！
t_en: Emmrich jumps up with a jerk, his fist almost colliding with your knee! So close!

t_ch: *皮皮西族粗口*特拉维斯那个叛徒！败类！皮皮西族之耻！
t_en: *Pepeshi curse* Travis! That traitor! Scumbag! A disgrace among us Pepeshi!

t_ch: 凡事都要阻挠我，装什么清高！发明家想靠自己的专利挣钱有错吗？啊？！
t_en: Why does he keep trying to mess with me, acting all high and mighty!? What's the big deal if an inventor wants to cash in on their patents, huh?

t_ch: 等着瞧吧，等艾默里奇的商业帝国拔地而起…老子绝对要让那个叛徒吃不了兜着走！
t_en: Just you wait... Once my business empire takes off... I'll make that traitor pay for his misdeeds!

t_ch: 「叮」——几枚艾迪恩游戏币从艾默里奇的口袋里掉了出来。
t_en: With a jingle, a few Aideen Tokens fall out of Emmrich's pocket.

t_ch: 它们现在归你了。拿走眼前这个家伙的东西完全不会令你觉得愧疚。
t_en: The tokens are yours now. Taking stuff from this guy won't make you feel guilty one bit.

t_ch: 艾默里奇突然蹦了起来，捏紧的小拳头差点锤到你的膝盖——好险！
t_en: Emmrich jumps up with a jerk, his fist almost colliding with your knee! So close!

t_ch: *皮皮西族粗口*特拉维斯那个叛徒！败类！皮皮西族之耻！
t_en: *Pepeshi curse* Travis! That traitor! Scumbag! A disgrace among us Pepeshi!

t_ch: 凡事都要阻挠我，装什么清高！发明家想靠自己的专利挣钱有错吗？啊？！
t_en: Why does he keep trying to mess with me, acting all high and mighty!? What's the big deal if an inventor wants to cash in on their patents, huh?

t_ch: 等着瞧吧，等艾默里奇的商业帝国拔地而起…老子绝对要让那个叛徒吃不了兜着走！
t_en: Just you wait... Once my business empire takes off... I'll make that traitor pay for his misdeeds!

t_ch: 为什么…为什么明明我发明出了那么多好东西，大家还是不重视我？
t_en: Why... Why is it that people still don't take me seriously, even with all the awesome stuff I've invented?

t_ch: 我的要求明明不高…我只希望能得到尊重！当然，最好还能多赚点钱……
t_en: I'm not asking for much... All I want is a bit of respect! And, of course, some money, if possible...

t_ch: 艾默里奇陷入失落之中。虽然有些于心不忍，但你觉得让他保持这种状态对整个匹诺康尼而言是件好事。
t_en: Emmrich falls into a state of despair. While this is somewhat disheartening, keeping him in this state may be advantageous for the welfare of Penacony.

t_ch: 为什么…为什么明明我发明出了那么多好东西，大家还是不重视我？
t_en: Why... Why is it that people still don't take me seriously, even with all the awesome stuff I've invented?

t_ch: 我的要求明明不高…我只希望能得到尊重！顶多，顶多还有赚点小钱……
t_en: I'm not asking for much... All I want is a bit of respect! And... and maybe some money, if possible...

t_ch: 你…你！你觉得我怎么样？你…你觉得老艾默里奇值不值得尊重？
t_en: You... You! What do you honestly think of me? Do... do you think ol' Emmrich deserves some respect?

t_ch: 你是个很有天赋的发明家。
t_en: You're an incredibly talented inventor.

t_ch: …谢谢，谢谢你能这么说！
t_en: ...Thank you. Thank you for your kind words!

t_ch: 拿去…这些你都拿去吧！愿意对我展现善意的人可不多…我愿意用这些换你的尊重！
t_en: Here... take all the money! Not so many people shower me with kindness... I'll trade the money for your respect!

t_ch: 你就是个守财奴。
t_en: You're just a miser.

t_ch: …你、原来你和他们都一样！
t_en: You... You're just like everyone else!

t_ch: 对，我是喜欢钱…但这世上有人不爱财吗？凭我的才能和天赋难道还换不来你们这种人的尊重吗？
t_en: Yeah, I love money... But is there anyone who doesn't? With all my talents and skills, shouldn't I deserve the respect of you people?
